prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (H2C=O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2. Anion state: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Geometry: user-supplied fixed .xyz (no re-optimization). Partial charges: Mulliken (PySCF). All three SCFs (N, N+1, N−1) converged. integrity.trustworthy = true. No warnings reported by the engine.

Headline result: f+ (electrophilic susceptibility) for C (atom 2) = 0.70839 — the most electrophilic site. f- (nucleophilic susceptibility) for O (atom 1) = 0.46742 — the most nucleophilic site. Dual descriptor for C = +0.59595 (strongly electrophilic). Full per-atom Fukui indices: f+(O)=0.46814, f+(C)=0.70839, f+(H3)=-0.088269, f+(H4)=-0.088260; f-(O)=0.46742, f-(C)=0.11244, f-(H3)=0.21008, f-(H4)=0.21006; dual(O)=+0.00071, dual(C)=+0.59595, dual(H3)=-0.29835, dual(H4)=-0.29832. Charge conservation: sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui ... --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1. Output: formaldehyde_fukui_dft.json.
